summary: Tested NXT8 LandingView (new) end-to-end: all sections, tickers, agent swipe cards, tariffs, Hermes inline chat (text + voice toggle), pilot CTA + skip → localStorage write + dashboard handoff. Backend POST /api/hermes/chat with landing payload returned 200 with non-empty `content` (~56s real LLM response). Regression checks for ?skip_landing=1 and OPS 6-widget view passed.

minor.issue: Real LLM call latency ~56s for first reply. Within the 60s SLA but slow; consider streaming or shorter system prompt for landing visitors.

critical_code_review_comments: LandingView.jsx (894 lines) is approaching the 700-line guideline — consider splitting Hero/AgentsSwipe/HermesChat/Tariffs/HowItWorks/Pilot into separate files under /views/landing/ for maintainability.

goToCheckout() opens hardcoded https://nxt8.pro/checkout — acceptable per user choice, but extract CHECKOUT_BASE to an env var (e.g. REACT_APP_CHECKOUT_BASE_URL) so non-production previews can stub it.

VoiceModeStub does not call api.voiceConverse in tests (mic gated). Lib reference `api.voiceConverse` was assumed present in lib/api.js — verify the export exists to avoid runtime ReferenceError when a user clicks the mic.

App.js conditional render uses CSS `hidden` class to toggle visibility of dashboard while landing is open. Both DOM trees are mounted simultaneously, meaning the dashboard's seed call and alerts polling run during landing. Consider lazy-mounting the dashboard only after onEnter() to save initial load cost.
HermesChat send() has no abort / cancellation on unmount — if the user clicks skip while a request is in flight, setState on unmounted component will log a warning.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Landing flow fully functional. All data-testids match TZ. window.open stubbing works for verifying CTAs without navigating to external nxt8.pro. localStorage key `nxt8_landing_seen` set to '1' on skip/pilot click. Note: my initial app-root-visible check was a false-positive (substring match on 'overflow-hidden') — the dashboard is correctly shown when class list does NOT contain the standalone 'hidden' utility. Real LLM reply observed in 56s; budget at least 60s for the assistant bubble.
